context: STA1 Diameter Origin-Host identity tied to Tango Networks UK (MNC053/MCC234); reused as entry node for Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR) location queries from 2023-03 through 2024-11; DNS-resolvable and IR.21-valid (not spoofed) suggesting long-term commercial access rather than a compromise.

STA1 Diameter Origin-Host identity tied to 019Mobile Israel; used as Proxy 1 entry point for IDR bursts on 2024-11-25; parent Origin-Realm (epc.mnc019.mcc425.3gppnetwork.org) returns NXDOMAIN across IPX DNS servers, a deliberate resolution suppression.

STA1 Diameter Edge Agent (DEA) hostname belonging to AIS Thailand IR.21 filings; paired with a spoofed China Unicom Origin-Realm to steer traffic through the Syniverse IPX. Cross-operator Origin-Host/Origin-Realm pairing violates 3GPP/GSMA standards.
STA2 fixed Diameter Route-Record entry host via Airtel Jersey (Channel Islands); reused across every observed STA2 attack as the single interconnect path into the IPX backbone.

019Mobile Origin-Realm domain; consistently returns NXDOMAIN on authoritative IPX DNS despite the underlying IP space (185.24.204.0/29 and .8/29, ASN 51825) being live and BGP-reachable via Partner Communications (AS12400). DNS-suppression-as-evasion CTI anchor.

STA1 Global Title used to open the 2024-11-25 campaign with two blocked sendRoutingInfoForSM (SRI-SM) attempts, then reused minutes later for provideSubscriberInfo (PSI) and anyTimeInterrogation (ATI) location queries.

STA1 Global Title reused across the PSI burst and the anyTimeInterrogation (ATI) escalation phase (11:17 GMT) on 2024-11-25; routing showed a mismatch against the IR.21-expected interconnect provider (Deutsche Telekom expected, BICS observed).

Global Title reused by STA1 (2024-11-25 PSI burst) and independently by STA2 (2025-02-11 SS7 reconnaissance PSI plus mt-ForwardSM SIMjacker delivery). A shared-identity overlap that is genealogy-relevant, not proof of a single actor.

Network owner of the 019Mobile signalling IP space; BGP AS-path 12400 -> 51825 confirms live reachability through Partner Communications despite the paired DNS domain being suppressed. Use for BGP/route-table hunting, not a blocklist entry.
Reconnaissance primitive used to resolve a target MSISDN to an IMSI prior to location queries; first phase of the STA1 chronology, blocked by the target signalling firewall on 2024-11-25.
Core SS7 location-disclosure primitive returning Cell ID, LAC and subscriber state; used by both STA1 (7-operator burst in 3 minutes) and STA2 (single probe before the SIMjacker SMS).

Escalation-tier SS7 location command sent by STA1 after 25 failed Diameter IDR attempts (12 messages from the same GT cluster, 11:17-11:20 GMT 2024-11-25). ATI is a stronger and rarer query than PSI, a high-value detection anchor precisely because legitimate use is uncommon.

value: Diameter Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR) with cross-realm Origin-Host/Origin-Realm mismatch
context: STA1 evasion signature: Origin-Host formatted for one operator (e.g. AIS Thailand DEA) paired with an Origin-Realm belonging to a different operator (China Unicom) to bypass firewall allow-lists keyed on realm.

value: SIMjacker binary SMS marker TP-PID=127 (0x7F) + TP-DCS=22 (0x16)
context: STA2 zero-click delivery signature: TP-PID 127 routes the SMS to the SIM card (not the handset UI) and TP-DCS 22 flags the payload as binary S@T-browser STK bytecode; device never displays or stores the message. Originally disclosed by Enea in 2019 (SIMjacker); observed reused by STA2 on 2025-02-11 via GT 467647531812 (Telenabler AB Sweden).
source: Citizen Lab Report No. 192 (Bad Connection); Enea SIMjacker disclosure (2019)

context: STA2 SIMjacker delivery SMS spoofed its TP-OA as an Airtel Rwanda subscriber number. SMS sender-field spoofing used to make the SIM Toolkit push look like ordinary inter-operator traffic.

value: Iran (MOIS/IRGC-nexus) SS7 location-tracking campaign against US military personnel in Iraq and Bahrain
context: Financial Times / Mobile Surveillance Monitor (Gary Miller) reporting that Iran exploited SS7 (the same protocol class documented in STA1/STA2, not the same confirmed infrastructure) to geolocate US military personnel at bases and hotels in Iraq and Bahrain during the build-up to and early days of the Iran War (approx. Feb-Mar 2026), enabling kinetic strikes that wounded over 150 US troops. This is a SEPARATE investigation from Citizen Lab STA1/STA2, do not treat as the same confirmed actor or infrastructure; see Attribution and confidence.
